title: Add an issues (open/closed) panel to a dashboard

blocks.content: In this milestone, you create a new dashboard and add a panel that displays GitHub issues data, including the count of open and closed issues over time.
Navigate to **Dashboards** from the main menu.

Open the panel editor by doing one of the following:

- If you see an **Add visualization** button or placeholder, click it.
- If you see a **Configure visualization** button, click it.
- If you see a **+** icon in the top toolbar, click it and select **Configure**.

Any of these options opens the panel editor.
Click the **data source** dropdown and select your GitHub data source from the list. If you have multiple data sources, look for the one named after your GitHub connection.
If you see a **New query editor** banner with a **Back to classic** button, click **Back to classic**. The GitHub data source requires the classic query editor to show the Query Type dropdown.
In the query editor at the bottom of the screen, find the **Query Type** dropdown and choose **Issues**.
Enter your GitHub organization name in the **Owner** field. For example, `grafana`.
Enter your repository name in the **Repository** field. For example, `grafana`.
In the query editor, find the **Time Field** dropdown and select **Created At**.

blocks.reftarget: [data-testid='data-testid Panel editor option pane field input Title']
blocks.content: In the **Panel options** on the right, enter a title for your panel in the **Title** field. For example, `GitHub Issues (Open/Closed) Overview`.
Click **Save dashboard**. In the dialog that appears, enter a title for your dashboard and click **Save**.
Click **Back to dashboard** to exit the panel editor and return to the dashboard view.

The panel now displays a Table visualization showing issue trends over time.

In the next milestone, you'll create a similar panel for pull request data.
